text.title: file-reference-passing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for file-reference-passing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Stage 1 returns "summary + content of relevant items"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Content is duplicated across stages, consuming tokens in both stage 1's output and stage 2's input. The summary itself becomes context-debt. Return refs only; stage 2 re-fetches on demand.

failure-mode.description: Subagent returns the entire file it analyzed
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Parent context blows up, especially if the subagent returns many files. The parent must parse and summarize-again. Instead, subagent should return a summary + list of paths, and parent re-reads what it needs.

failure-mode.description: Refs are LLM-generated paths that don't exist
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hallucinated refs break loading and waste downstream compute. Constrain refs to a manifest (enum or regex) that the LLM was explicitly given, or validate refs post-hoc and retry with a correction prompt.

failure-mode.description: Refs are remote URLs that expire
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A rerun days later fails because the URL no longer resolves. Snapshot the resource at the time of reference and reference the snapshot instead. Use timestamped URLs or immutable refs.

failure-mode.description: Pipeline persists raw content between steps
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Disk and memory both bloat as the corpus grows. State files become large and slow to load. Instead, persist only refs (tiny) and resolve at use time. This also allows the corpus to refresh without invalidating the pipeline state.
